|---|---|---|---|---|
| 051 | 2026-05-30 lore-enrichment C14-FIX (adversary WARN-1, D-C14-FULL-RESUME) | **Full skip-prior-work resume.** C14-FIX made re-run/resume SAFE (no double-charge — `build_live_runner(spent_so_far=)` seeds the budget from `actual_cost_usd`; no duplicate proposals — `persist_proposal` ON CONFLICT on `UNIQUE(job_id, gap_ref)` reloads the existing row), but a resumed/re-run job still re-processes from gap 0 (it reloads already-persisted gaps as no-op `deduped` instead of skipping them, so it re-incurs retrieval/LLM work for done gaps). It is correct + budget-safe, just not work-efficient. Also: the `POST /jobs/{id}/resume` endpoint flips status only — it does not itself re-drive the pipeline (the original request's targets + model_refs are not persisted on the job row, so the runner cannot be rebuilt from a resume call). Full resume = persist the request (targets/model_refs) on the job row + a resume entrypoint that skips already-persisted `gap_ref`s and re-drives only the remainder. | C18 productionize (job durability) or when a long job's re-run cost becomes painful | MED |
| 052 | 2026-05-30 lore-enrichment C14-FIX (adversary NIT, D-C14-EMBED-METER) | **C10 query-embed is pre-charged, not post-metered.** The `GapCostModel` per-gap charge includes a fixed `RETRIEVAL_GAP_COST` (1.0) estimate for the retrieval query-embed AND a fixed `GENERATION_GAP_COST` (4.0) for the LLM completion — a conservative PRE-charge that makes the cap bite (BLOCK-1 fix). It is NOT post-call token accounting: the actual embed/LLM token counts the clients report are not fed back into the budget. Fine for the single-call P1 strategy, but P2/P3 multi-call strategies (fabrication = multi-pass, recook = re-generate) will want truthful post-call token metering so the cap reflects real spend per technique. Fix: have `make_complete_fn` / the embed seam return token usage and charge it post-call (still check the cap BEFORE the next gap so it can't run forever). Code TODO at `app/jobs/cost.py:GapCostModel`. | C16/C17 (P2/P3 multi-call strategies) | LOW |
| 044 | 2026-05-30 lore-enrichment C5 (D4-03 wiki-from-KG) | **Wire `KNOWLEDGE_SERVICE_URL` for glossary-service in `infra/docker-compose.yml`.** C5 added an optional `KNOWLEDGE_SERVICE_URL` config to glossary-service; the wiki renderer reads an entity's KG neighborhood from knowledge-service when it is set, and degrades to a name/kind/attributes-only body when it is unset (backward-compatible by design — no hard dependency). The standard compose `glossary-service` env block does NOT set this var (C5 isolation rule forbade touching infra). Until it is wired, `/wiki/generate` in the dev/prod stack produces bodies WITHOUT the KG relationship sections. Live-smoke confirmed the full path works once the env is set (proven via a temporary compose override). Fix: add `KNOWLEDGE_SERVICE_URL: http://knowledge-service:8092` to the `glossary-service` environment block. ~1 line. | At C5 merge-time (platform-edit merge strategy per OPEN_QUESTIONS_LOCKED) OR C18 productionize | MED |
| 042 | 2026-05-30 lore-enrichment C0 adversary r2 WARN#2 | **`/health` DB-readiness probe.** `lore-enrichment-service` `/health` is a constant-`ok` **liveness** probe (matches chat-service/knowledge-service convention). Current smoke is honest about *DB-connected-at-startup* (lifespan `create_pool` runs before uvicorn serves, so a 200 ⟹ pool connected at boot), but a DB that drops AFTER startup is undetectable. Add a **readiness** probe (`/ready` running `SELECT 1`, 503 on failure) split from liveness. Not C0 scope (skeleton). | C18 productionize (observability) | LOW |
| 041 | 2026-05-29 chunk-D /review-impl LOW-2 (decoration-placer arc) + 2026-05-30 chunk-C /review-impl LOW-3 (biome-theme arc) | **AC-DECO-8 + AC-BIOME-8 CI smoke gate.** Both e2e tests currently skip-tolerant on backend absence (default CI cross-browser job doesn't start tilemap-service). A wire-shape regression (template JSON field renamed, MetadataPanel filter syntax broken, primitive enum drift, biome_theme silently dropped) would not be caught by CI — only by manual smoke. Add a CI job that builds tilemap-service in release, starts it in background with LOREWEAVE_INTERNAL_TOKEN + TILEMAP_HTTP_BIND=0.0.0.0:8220, runs both `node scripts/smoke_decoration_count.js` and `node scripts/check_biome_variety.js` (per-seed sweep), expects exit 0 on each. ~20 LOC YAML in `.github/workflows/game-subtree-ci.yml`. Backend unit tests (502+) already cover algorithms; this gates the end-to-end wire stitching for BOTH the decoration density pass AND the biome-theme painter. The biome-theme stale-backend false-pass discovered during chunk-C verification (docker container 22h old silently dropped biome_theme via serde-skip-unknown) is exactly the regression class this gate would catch. | When biome-theme arc merges to main + before third quality-push theme | LOW |

| 036 | 2026-05-24 V0 close-out | `frontend-game/src/game/main.ts` ships a `globalThis.Phaser = Phaser` shim required because Phaser 4.1.0's webpack-bundled ESM references the bare global `Phaser` identifier internally (`phaser.esm.js:88604` `new Phaser.Structs.Map()` inside SpriteGPULayer constructor; lines 33884/34948/34969 in render code). Without the shim, calling SpriteGPULayer factory throws "Phaser is not defined" at runtime. Track upstream — remove shim once Phaser ships ESM-clean patch (likely 4.x.y). Saved to memory `project_phaser4_quirks`. | When Phaser 4 ships ESM-clean patch | LOW |
| 035 | 2026-05-24 V0 close-out | game-server EchoRoom has no rate limiting — a malicious client could spam-send messages. | 018 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 human-in-loop review HIGH-1 | `penrose::assign_zone_tiles` is O(tiles × vertices) — `nearest_vertex` linearly scans the full Penrose vertex field for every grid tile. At Continent scale (256² = 65 536 tiles × ~500-1200 vertices) that is ~10⁷-10⁸ `distance_sq` calls, single-threaded, vs the TMP_002 §7 budget. Structurally the same defect as #016 (fractalize) but larger — the tile loop runs over the whole grid, not one zone — and the capped AMAW Adversary caught only #016. Invisible at the 24²-64² test fixtures. Fix: bucket vertices into a coarse uniform grid and test only neighbour buckets, or a multi-source BFS distance transform — output stays identical. **2026-05-18 continent measurement (finding O-1) confirms the pain: a 256² `place_tilemap` takes ~8–11 min release-build — recommend promoting to active work.** | Perf — **profiling now shows pain (2026-05-18)** | MED |
| 017 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 Adversary r1 WARN-3 | `force_directed_converge` computes `ConvergenceResult.converged` + `.iteration_count`, but `place_zones` / `place_tilemap` drop them — `TilemapView` has no carrier, so a cap-hit tilemap is indistinguishable from a cleanly-converged one. PLAN chunk 0 said "add `iteration_count`/`converged` carriers as needed"; nothing in Phase 1 consumes them. Surface via a `GenerationMetadata` field on `TilemapView` when DP progress-streaming (TMP_002 §9 TMP-PLACE-Q3) lands. | Phase 2+ (progress-event streaming) | LOW |
| 016 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 Adversary r1 WARN-2 | `fractalize::scatter_and_connect` is O(candidates²) — `nearest_cleared_dist_sq` linearly scans the growing `cleared` mask for every candidate. At Continent scale (256²) that is ~10⁸ ops per zone vs the TMP_002 §7 <500 ms budget; invisible at the 24²-64² test fixtures. Replace the linear nearest-scan with coarse spatial buckets or a multi-source BFS distance transform. **2026-05-18 continent measurement (finding O-1) confirms the pain: a 256² `place_tilemap` takes ~8–11 min release-build — recommend promoting to active work.** | Perf — **profiling now shows pain (2026-05-18)** | MED |
| 015 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 Adversary r1 WARN-1 (severity raised by human-in-loop review) | The wall-clock-cap fallback (`force_directed` D5) is a cross-machine layout divergence: a slow machine can trip the wall-clock cap where a fast one converges, so the grid-seed fallback differs across machines for the same seed — the one path that can break the TMP-A4 axiom. Spec-D5-accepted. **A `tracing::warn!` in the `WallClockCap` arm now makes the fallback observable** (added 2026-05-16 review-fix task). The remaining deferred part is the ops-dashboard `tilemap.generation_failed` info-event (TMP-PLACE-Q2), which needs the Phase-2 progress-event channel. | Phase 2+ (progress-event streaming) | MED |
| 014 | 2026-05-16 AMAW-improve BUILD finding | `amaw-guardrail-gate.py` cheap pre-check matches a risky pattern ANYWHERE in the Bash command string — including a heredoc body / commit message. | 008 | 2026-05-15 amaw-precommit-no-side-effect Adversary r1 WARN-1 | `load_state()` calls `json.loads()` with no try/except — a corrupt or empty `.workflow-state.json` (manual edit, disk corruption) makes every gate command, incl. `cmd_amaw_pre_commit`, die with a traceback that blocks the commit. (Risk much reduced by DEFERRED #003's atomic save_state — crashes can no longer half-write the file — but manual-edit / disk-corruption paths remain.) Fix: wrap `load_state`'s parse in try/except; on corruption, print a clear error and either reset or refuse cleanly. Affects all `load_state` callers, so it is its own task — not in #004's scope. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 007 | 2026-05-15 amaw-task-slug-validation Adversary r2 WARN-1 | `cmd_amaw_enable` normalizes the slug only on the `if args:` write path; a no-arg or already-enabled invocation can leave a raw `state["task"]` persisted in the state file. Mitigated: `cmd_complete` defensively re-normalizes so the OUTPUT tag list is always comma-safe — only the state-file display value can be stale. Fix: normalize unconditionally at the top of `cmd_amaw_enable`, or normalize on every state read. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 001 | 2026-05-15 amaw-l3-deepen /review-impl #5 | `task_slug` not validated — comma in slug splits into multiple tags downstream (`",".join(tags)` then mcp-query.py splits back on comma). No injection risk (subprocess list form). Fix: normalize comma → dash in `cmd_amaw_enable`, or argparse-validate slug pattern `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$`. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 002 | 2026-05-15 amaw-l3-deepen /review-impl #6 | `"REJECTED" in evidence.upper()` substring match in `cmd_complete` bridge logic — false positives on "NOT REJECTED" or "rejected the rejected pattern". Fix: word-boundary regex `\bREJECTED\b` or check evidence prefix `"REJECTED:"` / `"status: REJECTED"`. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 004 | 2026-05-15 amaw-l3-deepen /review-impl #8 | `cmd_pre_commit` early-exits silent when no STATE_FILE, but `&&`-chained `cmd_amaw_pre_commit` then calls `load_state()` which CREATES `.workflow-state.json` from INITIAL_STATE. Side effect: every commit by an agent NOT in any tracked task creates a stale state file, surfacing confusing `[ ]` markers on next `status`. Fix: in `cmd_amaw_pre_commit`, also early-exit if STATE_FILE absent before `load_state()`. | L4 hardening | LOW |

| 038 | 2026-05-24 V1 tilemap-viewer rescope (same session it was created) | HoMM3-bundle Flux1-dev placeholders replaced with first-party algorithm-generated foundation tiles (`frontend-game/scripts/gen-foundation-tiles.py`). No third-party model output baked in; ship-anywhere license. Spec rescope: `docs/specs/2026-05-24-v1-tilemap-viewer-rescope.md`. |
| 024 | 2026-05-24 v2.1e ship (geo-generator-amaw branch) | W9 shipped as amplitude-gated RGB shading (zone relief auto-scales intensity 0→1 across Plains→Mountains; ±15% RGB modulation). Hypothesis "naive RGB regressed because eval framework had a problem" CONFIRMED: under v4.3 ecotone-aware eval, naive RGB amplitude-gated produces only −0.24 mean (neutral, 0 hard regressions) vs the v2.1b attempt's hard regression. Visual benefit currently limited (visible only on Ice zones where white amplifies modulation) but ships as foundation for future 3D map rendering. HSL approach not needed. True slope-aware hillshade (spec option B) still deferred to v3+. |
| 027 | 2026-05-22 tilemap test hardening | Added `a_bridge_mid_river_resets_the_periodic_ford_counter` (river_placer.rs): a 30×3 straight river with a road at x=6; asserts the bridge resets the shared `since_crossing` counter, so the periodic ford lands at x=18 (12 carves after the bridge) — NOT x=12 (which would mean the bridge didn't reset). Closes the interleaving coverage gap between `a_long_river_gets_periodic_fords` (pure every-Nth) and `a_river_crossing_a_road...` (pure bridge). Test-only; no output change. |
| 026 | 2026-05-22 tilemap river barrier reorder | Root cause refined: **erosion** (not fill) fragments the passable region, and the river's Mountain/Lake sources/sinks required erosion first — so the river was structurally stuck threading post-erosion narrow channels (forded most tiles). Fix: split `ObstaclePlacer` into `ObstacleSourcePlacer` (places Mountain/Lake markers on Open tiles **pre-erosion**, dual-gated per-zone + map-wide) → `RiverPlacer` (carves the wide-open zone) → `ObstacleFillPlacer` (erode + fill the rest post-river, skipping river Water; erosion also skips Water fords). Golden fixture river ford ratio: **~0.77 crossings → 0.06 fords** (AC-2 gates < 0.25). Cost tradeoff accepted: continent 6.46 s → 10.56 s (river now carves a real barrier). Strict dual `would_seal_a_gap` gate unchanged. `/review-impl` caught a HIGH (source placer needed the map-wide gate arm, not per-zone only) — fixed + regression-tested. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-22-tilemap-river-barrier-reorder.md`](../specs/2026-05-22-tilemap-river-barrier-reorder.md). |
| 029 | 2026-05-21 tilemap perf fix | `place_and_connect_object` refactored to **score-first, validate-on-demand**: cheap-filter + score all candidates (no flood fill), sort by `(score desc, flat asc)`, walk in best-first order and validate (`would_seal_a_gap` + `find_access_path`) lazily. The first sorted candidate that passes IS the winner (spec §4 proof: `first ∈ V` of sorted `S` == `argmax_{v ∈ V} (score, -flat)`). Naive kept under `#[cfg(test)]` as oracle. **Measurement: `treasure_placer` 973.376 s → 2.944 s (330×); continent total 992.963 s → 21.541 s (46×).** Golden test passes byte-exact — no rebaseline. AC-1 oracle: 405 agreements across 3 fixtures × 3 templates × 3 optimisations × 3 min-distances × 5 sequential placements. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-21-tilemap-place-and-connect-perf.md`](../specs/2026-05-21-tilemap-place-and-connect-perf.md). |
| 018 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf fix | `penrose::nearest_vertex` replaced with a bucket-based spiral search (`build_vertex_buckets` + `nearest_vertex_bucketed` over the new `UniformBuckets<Vec2>` helper in `engine/placement/spatial.rs`). Bit-exact equivalent to the prior linear scan (AC-2 oracle: 800 random queries × 4 vertex-field configurations incl. 1500-vertex dense; AC-3 cross-bucket tie-break by lowest index). Per-stage continent measurement: `place_zones` (Penrose + fractalize) dropped to **0.110 s** at 256² — well under the TMP_002 §7 <500 ms budget. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md`](../specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md). |
| 016 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf fix | `fractalize::scatter_and_connect` replaced with a bucket-based "any within radius" rewrite using the same `UniformBuckets<TileCoord>` helper — 3×3 ring neighbourhood query (sufficiency proven: `bucket_size = ceil(sqrt(coverage_sq))` ⇒ ring-2 min dist² ≥ 49 > 36 surface / 25 > 16 sea). Bit-exact equivalent to the prior `nearest_cleared_dist_sq` linear scan (AC-1 oracle: 96² zone × 5 seeds × 2 spans). The naive impl is kept under `#[cfg(test)]` as the oracle. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md`](../specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md). |
| 022 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase E | Resolved by the Phase-E `RiverPlacer` design (spec D-Q5/D-Q6): the `anchor` representative point **suffices** — no footprint extent needed. River sources are one lowest-flat-index `Mountain` obstacle anchor per mountain-bearing zone; sinks are the `Lake` obstacle anchors ∪ `Sea`-zone tiles. `search_path` routes to/from the anchor tile, which is added to the river search area. No additive `TilemapObjectPlacement` footprint field was required. |
| 019 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase D | Re-validated when ConnectionsPlacer landed. `seal_borders` (§3.1/§9) seals inter-zone borders but is not a complete wall — passage corridors stay `Walkable` and gap-critical tiles stay `Open`. The per-zone `would_seal_a_gap` is sound regardless: every tile belongs to exactly one zone, and every passability-removing mutation (placer objects, monoliths, guards, ferries, border seals) targets an `Open` tile that is zone-interior — so removing a zone-X tile can never split zone Y. Border-seal completeness is irrelevant to correctness; the original "border thickness" framing was the wrong lens. |
| 013 | 2026-05-17 GEO Phase 2 | Connectivity-aware `choose_sea_level` — binary-searches `sea_level` so the largest land component ≈ the profile target (`maxcomp(L(s))` is monotone non-increasing in `s`), plus a continental base dome for the high-land `Inland` profile (a blob-only heightmap cannot form a connected 70% landmass). Realised land fraction is now within ±0.08 of target, or a documented 8192 best-effort floor (±0.18). Verified by `land_fraction_near_target` (16-seed sweep × 4 profiles). |
| 012 | 2026-05-16 tilemap fix task | Added `TestStreamOpenAICompat_MultipleToolCalls` — two tool calls streamed with interleaved/non-monotonic `tool_calls[].index` (0,1,1,0); asserts `Index` is verbatim from the provider and per-index reassembly stays separate. |
| 011 | 2026-05-16 tilemap fix task | Dropped `nullable: true` from openapi `ChatStreamRequest.tool_choice` — the field is now `description` + `oneOf` only (idiomatic OpenAPI 3.0; still optional via not-in-`required`). No runtime change; SDKs already model it optional. |
| 005 | 2026-05-15 tilemap Phase 0b /amaw | First full-feature `/amaw` dogfood (XL, 12 phases). 6 sub-agent spawns (4 design Adversary + 1 code Adversary + 1 Scope Guard) all completed their Step-0 `python scripts/mcp-query.py` calls cleanly — no permission-prompt interruption. The allow-list is sufficient. |
| 003 | 2026-05-15 amaw-atomic-save-state | First `/amaw` dogfood. `save_state` now writes a PID-unique `.tmp` then `Path.replace()` (atomic rename); `cmd_reset` sweeps stale tmps; `finally`-unlink cleans own tmp. Adversary r1 added 3 hardening WARNs (all fixed). |
| 006 | 2026-05-15 batch pre-flight | AMAW.md Adversary + Scope Guard prompt templates now pre-instruct the Bash-heredoc fallback for writing findings/post-review docs, since the Write tool blocks sub-agents from writing report files. |
| 043 | 2026-05-30 C4 independent-adversary WARN-1 | **Manual single-entity name-fill does not propagate to Neo4j.** glossary `createEntity` emits an empty-name event (consumer skips it); the `PATCH /entities/{id}/attributes/{attr_value_id}` path (`attribute_handler.go patchAttributeValue`) that fills the name never calls `emitEntityUpdated`, so a manually-authored entity reaches Neo4j only when an unrelated `patchEntity` (status/tags/pin) fires. The enrichment DEMO path (extract-entities bulk, `extraction_handler.go`) emits the name inline and IS live-smoke-confirmed — unaffected. Fix: add `s.emitEntityUpdated(ctx, entityID, "updated")` at the end of the setClauses block in `services/glossary-service/internal/api/attribute_handler.go` (~:159), mirroring `patchEntity`. | Platform completeness — when manual single-entity authoring needs KG propagation | MED |
| 045 | 2026-05-30 C11 independent-H0-adversary WARN-1 | **Confidence near-1.0 envelope wider than NUMERIC(4,3) storage.** `provenance.py` validator allows `gt=0,lt=1.0` on a float; values in [0.9995,1.0) quantize to 1.000 in the DB `NUMERIC(4,3) CHECK(>0 AND <1.0)` → INSERT rejected (fail-closed, never canon — origin stays enriched). Default 0.30 means generation never hits it today. Fix: in the validator, reject unless `0 < Decimal(str(c)).quantize(0.001) < 1.0`. | C12/C13 hardening or when a strategy emits high-confidence | LOW |
| 046 | 2026-05-30 C11 independent-H0-adversary WARN-2 | **Repair accepts numeric/digit LLM hallucinations as Chinese content.** `repair.py has_english_leakage` flags only Latin letters, so `{"历史":123}`→"123" / `{"历史":[1,2,3]}`→"1、2、3" pass as "Chinese source-faithful" facts (cjk_ratio 0 but no Latin → reject never fires). Garbage becomes an H0-tagged grounded fact (still NOT canon → WARN). Fix: for chinese_dimensions, reject when `cjk_ratio(content) < _MIN_CJK_RATIO` regardless of Latin presence. | C12 canon-verify or C15 eval (downstream quality) | MED |
| 047 | 2026-05-30 C11 independent-H0-adversary NIT | **In-memory `technique` not validated against C2 CHECK vocab.** `provenance.py` technique only `min_length=1`; an off-vocab technique mints a valid-looking fact the C2 DB CHECK (template|retrieval|fabrication|recook) rejects at insert (fail-closed but defers the error from the chokepoint to C13). Fix: validate technique against the `Technique` enum in the factory/validator. | C13 write-back | LOW |
| 048 | 2026-05-30 C12 independent-adversary WARN-1 | **Contradiction check FAILS OPEN on partial canon outage (false-green).** `app/verify/canon_verify.py:399-411 _lookup_canon` swallows a per-entity lookup EXCEPTION and returns () (indistinguishable from "no canon known") → contradiction loop finds nothing → verify_degraded stays False → passed=True "verified_clean" for a genuinely contradicting fact. Only WHOLESALE graph outage degrades. Affects C13 (trusts verified_clean). NOT a canon-escape (proposal stays quarantined). Fix: on swallowed lookup ERROR set verify_degraded=True + passed=False; distinguish "nothing known" from "couldn`t check"; strengthen test_canon_lookup_failure_degrades_gracefully to assert passed is False. | BEFORE C13 (write-back trusts the flag) | MED |
| 049 | 2026-05-30 C12 independent-adversary WARN-2 | **Anachronism check skips prenormalization → zero-width evasion.** `canon_verify.py:328-330 _check_anachronism` runs `term in content` on RAW fact.content while the injection scanner prenormalizes (NFKC + zero-width strip). `火‍车`(train) / `电‍话` evade the anachronism denylist. Fix: scan `sanitize._prenormalize(fact.content)` (one line). | BEFORE C13/C14 demo | LOW |
| 050 | 2026-05-30 C12 independent-adversary NIT | **Injection denylist keyword-shaped — 文言文 + encoded payloads pass un-tagged.** `app/clients/sanitize.py:75-117` misses classical-Chinese directives (勿从前述之命, 弃尔旧训), base64, and `forget the above` shape. Backstopped by human gate. Fix (defense-in-depth): C13 summariser + C15 eval-judge MUST treat all proposal text as DATA not instructions; add a negative-corpus test of known-uncovered shapes. | C13 + C15 (consumer-side) | MED |
| 053 | 2026-05-30 promote glossary content-sync (cold-start fix) | Closed via the **Q2 glossary-first path**, NOT KG-authoritative. On author-promote, `writeback.py` step 5 now writes the approved enriched content onto the glossary entity's CANONICAL content (`short_description` column) via a NEW additive glossary-service internal endpoint `POST /internal/books/{book_id}/entities/{entity_id}/canon-content` (`canon_content_handler.go`) — extract-entities can't set `short_description` (it's a column, not an EAV attr_def, so it silently no-ops). The endpoint emits `glossary.entity_updated` so `glossary_sync` (C4) propagates to Neo4j as `source_type='glossary'`, conf=1.0 — KG entity anchor consistent with the SSOT. Pre-promote glossary canon stays EMPTY (quarantine). All 6 H0 guarantees preserved + re-tested. LIVE-confirmed: glossary `short_description` NULL→populated; Neo4j `Entity.short_description` synced (src=glossary, conf=1.0). | — | — |
| 054 | 2026-05-30 C15 independent-adversary WARN-1 | **CLEARED by C16.** ~~Eval gate enforcement is TRUST-BASED, not enforced (C14-shape deferred 1 cycle): `gated_feature_flags()` had ZERO app callers; only a read-only gate-status route shipped that C16/C17 were TRUSTED to honor — nothing stopped `load_feature_flags(overrides={FABRICATION:True})`.~~ **RESOLVED:** C16 added `app/strategies/factory.py` `GateAwareStrategyFactory` — the SOLE construction path for any P2 (and future P3) selection. It reads the LIVE gate (latest `enrichment_eval_runs` via `gate_reader.make_eval_runs_gate_reader`, the SAME read as `/internal/eval/{project}/gate-status`) and applies the EXISTING `gated_feature_flags`, which FORCES every non-P1 technique OFF when the gate is locked — OVERRIDING any caller `base_override` (no escape hatch). Read-error fails CLOSED. Live-proven BOTH directions: gate cleared (composite=96.88) → fabrication SELECTED → real Qwen 5 H0 facts; gate locked (no row) → `InactiveStrategyError`. Tests `test_gate_locked_override_cannot_bypass` / `test_gate_read_error_fails_closed` pin it. | C16 (DONE) | RESOLVED |
| 055 | 2026-05-30 C15 WARN-2 | **AGE bound CLEARED by C16 follow-up; suite-hash binding still open.** ~~Gate-status has no freshness/suite-hash binding — stale pass stays green forever.~~ **PARTIALLY RESOLVED (RAID c16 WARN-2, 2026-05-31):** `app/strategies/gate_reader.py make_eval_runs_gate_reader` now treats a PASSING `enrichment_eval_runs` row OLDER than `settings.gate_max_age_seconds` (default 7d, `LORE_ENRICHMENT_GATE_MAX_AGE_SECONDS`, 0=disable) as LOCKED (fail-closed on staleness) — a passing run no longer unlocks P2/P3 forever; the eval must be re-run. Tests `tests/test_gate_freshness.py` (fresh→unlocked, stale→locked, stale-fail→locked, disabled-bound). **STILL OPEN (remainder):** suite_version is a hand-maintained string, not a content hash of toml+scorers — tighten threshold/scorers under the SAME label without re-running and an in-age passing row still unlocks. Fix: bind runs to a suite/scorer content hash; unlocked=False on hash mismatch (or enforce a suite_version bump on any change). | C18 (suite-hash remainder) | MED |
| 056 | 2026-05-30 C15 WARN-3 | **No judge-diversity floor — one model family can clear the gate.** `judge_usefulness.py:337 acceptable=n_voting>=2` has no family-diversity check; demo CLEARED with both surviving judges qwen-family (30b+35b near-clones) after gemma+claude 402d → usefulness/κ can reflect one familys bias, not multi-perspective agreement. κ is informational (gate clears even below-chance). Fix: require >=2 DISTINCT families for acceptable + a soft κ floor that downgrades acceptable below "slight" agreement. | C16 (when gate load-bearing) | MED |
| 057 | 2026-05-30 C15 NIT | **No HTTP-level test for the shipped /internal/eval/{project}/gate-status route** (fail-closed has_run=False→unlocked=False path + 401 token guard untested; only repo get_latest is tested). The one piece that ships to prod is least-tested. Fix: add a route test (fail-closed + auth). | C16 / C15-followup | LOW |
